metadata.title:
FIVE WOULON'


Be BOUND

HENCE THE COUNCIL LOCKS
ON CHARTER AMENDMENTS
AND SEVERAL OF THEM ARE
KNOCKED OUT.

pages.raw_text:
Five members of the city council
voted last night that they would
not be bound by the action of the
charter revision committee. These
FIVE men incurred the wrath of
Chairman Frank Post and James
Geraghty, and played little even
ing up game when they passed
President Lamberts resolution rob-
bling Geraghty of his job as counsel
for the park commission and its
salary of $1,000 year. That, of
course, is a terrific blow to an at
torney of Geraghty's practice.


There were sparks flying from
the moment the charter amend
ments were called up. Councilman
Gray showed his hand at the out
set by sparring for time, but was
defeated Then Councilman Schil-
Ter played into the hands of the
opposition by moving an amend
ment to the provision giving the
mayor more power, making the city
engineer a member of the board of
public works, abolishing the police
and fire commissions, and stating
how the chiefs of the police and
fire department were to be named.


The fight was over the city engi-
neer. The council took the posi-
ton that the man who drew the
plans for improvements should not
be the man to finally pass upon
them. 1t was also held that the
chief of police should be a civil
service position and not a political
one.


About this time Chairman Post
stated that the council had bound
itself to report favorably on every
amendment offered. Then the
storm broke. Councilman Mohr,
who talked every time he could
get recognition and took keen de-
light in reiterating that he was not
bound, led the fight. He insisted
7 he was not bound by the action of
the commission, and that he would
vote as he thought best. He OBJ
5ected to a commission appointed
by one man coming into the city
government and telling the council
what 1t was forced to do President
Lambert and Councilman Dalke
took the same position, insisting
they were not bound. Chairman
Post came back with hot shot, and
James Geraghty also fred a broad
side or two.
